mechanism_narrative: A sustained reduction in the federal funds rate over the prior three months lowers the risk-free discount rate, which increases the present value of future corporate cash flows and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of observed easing months over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month jump in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points triggers a repricing of recession risk, causing institutional investors to reduce equity exposure and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered months, S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns more than 50 percent of the time, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in real retail sales and a decline in payroll employment signal a broad demand shock that reduces expected revenues across the corporate sector, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and PAYEMS decline month-over-month, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflationary pressure, a month-over-month decline in industrial production signals supply-driven margin compression that causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.4 percent and INDPRO declines, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent downward trend in new housing starts reflects weakening construction activity and household formation, which drags on aggregate demand and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST 3-month rolling change is negative while S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of those periods over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating growth in real retail sales indicates robust consumer spending that boosts near-term earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth rate increases by more than 1.0 percent
failure_condition: In months when RSAFS growth accelerates by more than 1.0 percent, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals reduced manufacturing profitability and capacity utilization, leading to negative earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in INDPRO coincide with positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals an economy-wide contraction in aggregate demand that causes broad-based earnings declines and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is negative, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of quarters over a 10-year rolling window.
